note: Verbatim phrases of the locked narration, in spoken order, split at the script's <break> tags and sentence/clause boundaries (<=~95 chars). Block windows were measured by normalized FFT cross-correlation of each WC-09-01-sNN.mp3 against the joined master (8 kHz mono PCM, numpy rfft; NCC score 1.000 for all 12). The Σ(ffprobe audio+0.4) formula predicts 230.42s but the ffprobed master is 229.956s (0.46s off, past the ±0.3s gate). Cross-correlation shows the master is the 12 blocks concatenated with an exact 0.400s gap after each; per-file ffprobe durations are inflated ~0.0386s each by mp3 encoder-delay padding that the PCM concat strips (12×0.0386≈0.46s), which is the whole discrepancy. Each block startS is its measured onset (an exact multiple of the 0.08s mp3-frame grid); final end = ffprobed master duration 229.956s.

phrases.text: Open the Digital module.
The sidebar splits in two.
The top group, Customer Portal, runs the surfaces your members sign into.
Five entries.
The mobile pages sit below, but those are the next lesson.
Click Portal Members.
This is your roster of everyone who holds a portal login.
When a customer says the website won't let them in, this is where you start.
Four numbers across the top.
Registered members, how many were active this month, how many joined this week,
and the share who reach you on mobile.
One glance sizes the portal and shows how live it is.
That mobile share matters, since most portal traffic now comes through the app.
Below sit three cards.
Growth over the year, the plan mix with unlimited monthly the biggest slice,
and account health split into active, inactive, and suspended.
Watch that suspended slice;
it's where locked-out members surface before they call.
Now the roster itself.
New Record and Export sit top-right, a search box and Filters just under them.
Today you're only reading, so leave Export alone.
It sends data out, and this is a look-only tour.
Six columns.
Name, email, when they joined, their plan, portal status, and last login.
Portal status is your first stop when someone can't reach the website:
it tells you whether their access is even active.
Last login backs it up, showing when they were really here.
Down at the foot: rows per page, a record count, and the pager.
Reading, paging, and searching change nothing.
New Record would open a create form,
but that form didn't load during verification, so we leave it parked.
Back to the sidebar.
Click Booking Page.
The admin chrome disappears.
What loads is the customer's own booking site,
with its own branding and a five step wizard that runs from service to confirm.
Step one offers three service categories: an inspection, an oil change, and detailing.
Read that first screen, then stop.
Advancing the wizard books a real visit, so Continue never gets clicked.
Portal Messages next.
This is where member messages land and get worked.
Fourteen sit unread right now, twenty eight are open,
and responses average around three hours.
Every row carries a status, a priority, and an owner,
so a billing question and a cancellation request never look alike.
Replying reaches a real customer.
We read the list and touch nothing.
Portal Billing sits just under it.
A running log of billing events on the portal side.
Card updates, plan changes, auto renewals,
and the failures that turn into phone calls.
When a member asks why a card was declined,
the action and status columns tell that story in one row.
Every line here is live money.
Read it, always; touch it, never.
Last entry, Portal Notifications.
The automated sends.
A welcome email fires when an account is created,
and a renewal reminder goes out a week ahead.
The list tracks each one's audience, open rate, and status.
Twelve are active, and more than eight hundred went out today alone.
These reach real members, so nothing here is ever sent, created, or edited in training.
So that's the Customer Portal group.
Five entries, and you have now read all five.
Portal Members for the roster and login trouble.
The booking page for what the customer actually sees.
Messages for the inbox, Billing for the money log, Notifications for the automated sends.
Reach them from the sidebar, since a pasted address still fails.
And read everything without sending, booking, or exporting a thing.
